title: cousinia astracanica
intro: Cousinia astracanica, also known as Cousinia, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia, specifically in the Kazakhstan and Uzbekistan territories. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which help to soothe digestive issues and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Cousinia astracanica can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are taken internally to alleviate symptoms of indigestion, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal problems.
